description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crustacean, specifically resembling a crayfish or small lobster, with a segmented brown exoskeleton. The body is divided into a robust cephalothorax and a flexible, banded abdomen, both covered in a hard, textured shell. Two large, asymmetrical claws (chelae) extend from the front, with one claw noticeably larger than the other, and are covered in small bumps and spines. The crustacean has multiple jointed walking legs on each side, and two long, thin antennae protrude from the head region. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, providing a wide field of vision. The overall coloration is a mix of earthy browns with subtle hints of reddish and grayish tones, and the surface appears matte and slightly rough.
description: A brown, segmented crustacean with large claws, jointed legs, and long antennae, featuring a hard, textured shell and stalked eyes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the segmentation of the abdomen.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail fan, segmentation of the abdomen, and the arrangement of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the claws, antennae, and the curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, stalked eyes, and the forward-projecting antennae.
